reviews.review_description: Being able to hold a natural conversation with NPC's and having them react realistically to what you say is nothing short of magical, and must have taken an immense effort to get working well. At some point in the future it might feel normal for players to naturally talk to NPCs in any game, but its games like this that are laying the foundation now to get there. Super impressive!

Bought this yesterday. The whole family sits around watching each other interact with this game. My 10yo daughter ran home from school to get Cafe time. She is now telling James about a exploding space centepede that messes up robot circuits and he seems seriously concerned about it. Now she's asking about what is on the TV in the background and he's talking about it in real-time. And she ordered a orange juice with whipped cream and chocolate and he made it. We're just absolutely stunned. 

This game is like YEARS into the future. 

I love that it is safe for kids, with no IRL idiots running around cursing and bullying. I feel like it could help her with her ADHD because she really has to pay attention to the conversation and interact in real ways like an actual human except that they are patient and interested in everything she says (the podcaster is asking her what middle school is like)

It's a great experience and I want more of those kinds of interactions, with less rails than here. 

There's just a single mission which can be achieved in 2-3 hours, and then your only option to continue playing is to make a new save, with you having to do the same thing again like in a Groundhog day where you already know the NPCs and their personalities. Talks aren't fully free form since characters will want to steer you back at scripted quests and won't engage much in random topics that you float.

If it would have less rails and NPCs would have more world knowledge (they can't even explain how their world works and have very flat personalities), it would be something where you can sink hours just talking about stuff.

I like this game and the game was good, but in the end my wish you in the end of the game you can leave just like not leave leave the game himself but go to the shadow and after you can tell visor if you wanna start a new game or open another save I promise I had so much fun in the game that’s the best game I spend money on ￼

what a unique, fun game. just hand tracking and your voice, conversing with different NPC personalities in a cafe. They are really good at understanding your questions / requests. Just very good AI ! 

There are also interactable objects on every table in the cafe so you can like fiddle with stuff while talking.

5/5. perfect game for VR.



Also, I wonder; is the AI run locally on the headset, or streamed from a server?

I’m 55 and never thought I’d see the day where robots can carry on a conversation with you— and I do mean actually communicating with you. Thru this game, the robots respond to pretty random ideas very intricately.  I couldn’t get James to make me a multi-colored drink (he kept serving a white liquid and called it multi-colored) but other than that, brilliant use of A/I to create one of the first of its kind on Meta.

reviews.review_title: I think I might be friends with Debbie
reviews.review_description: Okay as someone who was super skeptical about this game on reveal after spending 4 hours playing through it I gotta say it just works. and it's amazing. Yes the ai will mess up but very rarely (misheard words or the voice being different) but it just feels so natural, I had genuine bonds with some of these robots, it really feels like you can just say anything, and you can just talk to the robots for hours without doing any of the tasks If you want, I love it. (Though not a fan of the robot that constantly said slang)

Warning: Spoiler alert! Read with your own discretion.

Game was an absolute blast and a brilliant experience overall. It has many characters varying one from another. Every one of them has different story and personality. However, I got really upset when I realized it's the end of the game and you can't interact with any robot that you want after patch day. So in my opinion when you finish the game you should be able to talk with any robot that you want as long as you'd like, because some of my conversations were really fun and I'd love to continue them without any objective being there. Nonetheless great game really, something fresh in the market.

 There are some little bugs like some rare misunderstandings or sudden change of tone but those are just small things I can ignore since the game's first version runs smooth and 99% of the time has no issues! 5 stars.

Something good! This game is a brand new concept, a one of a kind experience.
Really well made!
I enjoy chatting with the customers of the cafe, better conversations than I have had with some real people actually :)

A couple of tips for the devs.

The cafe needs a selection of background music, maybe a jukebox? Or at least we could ask James to change it for us.
I would like to be able to change the color of Visor if I ask her to.
A foxy humanoid droid would be welcome, something like from a Heavy Metal magazine cover.

Thanks for creating this!

reviews.review_title: The first in a new era of games.
reviews.review_description:
I know that sounds dramatic, but I genuinely think it's true. I've been keeping an eye on this game since it was announced, and man does it pay off. Its true that people are hostile to the usage of AI, and that I understand, but in games like this? This game would only be achievable with the usage of this new, awesome technology.

The game is simple, but it's the new use of AI that makes it so enticing! If this catches on, I'm sure this game will be seen as relatively niche moving forward, but it will always be the game that kicked off the inclusive use of AI in videogames.

Props to the developers! Awesome job!

This game was a fun, but short, experience. As a player, you’re taken to this interstellar café space where you will face several rounds of gameplay with a variety of robots. Each one has a task you need to help them complete, from creating a podcast show to writing marketing slogans. The goal is to get them all to attend a big party the bartender is hosting. ￼It was cute and the robots had some fun things to say. I just wish they was a bit more game there to make the experience a little longer.￼￼￼ Regardless, I’m glad to support Devs like this who are trying something different and new with VR medium. We see so many of the same types of games over and over again, and Meta makes it so difficult to find good games on the store these days. Give this one a shot if you’re looking for something different. ￼

I was so immersed in this.   Just the fact that I was having some level of conversation with these robots was surreal.   They do steer you towards the game objective, but they do pick up what you’re saying  and interpret it. ￼  I felt like I was stepping in to the future of games. Looking forward to more of these AI interactions.   I asked random questions about the Cafe and the view out the window and they nailed the right answer .
Totally mind blowing stuff .￼￼￼
This has to be one of many favorites on vr

reviews.review_title: I love it and have some suggestions
reviews.review_description: I really love this game where you can use your voice to talk but I have some suggestions first a Valentine’s Day update where you help robots find a lover.Or you play as the cafe worker not James but yeah James takes the time off so he lets you take peoples orders.Or just a multiplayer hangout with public and private servers and you can still talk to the robots and they can talk to you and your friends at 1 time.I think if you add one of these features the game will become way more popular.

It was fun for 5 seconds before the npc got annoying and cup holders wouldn't refill ice even though James said it would and I got bored try to leave got stuck in a visor screen and couldn't save so like an hour of kind of fun wasted.

£12 FOR THIS SLOP!!!

reviews.review_title: More of an experience than a game
reviews.review_description: This was really fun to play and the interactions felt real it is really impressive what they have accomplished here. Although it is very short. I finished it the same day I got it. But it is a good demonstration of the impressive technology.

I've been looking for a good AI game and this one certainly fits the bill. I gave it 5 stars, not because its the best game ive played this year, but because as an AI game its the best one I tried.  Easy conversations, with some clever responses and Very fast replies. Some AI games have such long reply times, and it's not the case here. I was very impressed playing this game and throughly enjoyed myself.  It is a short game, I think it took me 3 hours, but I still think its worth the money and looking forward to having another go through.

reviews.review_title: The first steps into the future
reviews.review_description:
The first five minutes this is really kind of mind boggling. Talking to visor to move around is really cool and then talking to the characters is sick that it works that smoothly. It really feels like a first step of what is going to be possible with NPCs in other games as well. 

The topics themselves were for me less interesting personally because I’m not that much of roleplayer and I need more of a clear goal in the game. But if you want to joke around this is a perfect game for that.

The polish of the game is also on point. It’s a nice environment to be in. Overall recommended as a really novel experience!

Genuinely so much better than I was expecting. Answers are fast, full of charm and the minigames are great fun. Not a game I could see myself playing a ton, but it’s interesting for sure, would recommend giving it a go.
One of the best robot cafés I ever teleported to! The robots are so kind and friendly, the drinks are amazingly tasty, and... wow. I'm gonna visit that café every day for the rest of my life!!
I tried most AI apps on the Quest, this is the first one that really impresses me. The conversations I had were so hilarious that I was laughing out loud. Well done Dev, well done.
If it uses AI instead of VI to interact with you, need to be up front and honestly about that in the description.
Got boring quick i want a refund

I saw A Wolf in VR play this game, and I wanted it immediately. I got super st*ned and started the game, beginning with the psychedelic spiritual robot. I got bad anxiety and had to turn the game off. I will try it again under normal circumstances.
I’m new to vr but I can say this is one of the creative games ever buy it if you are lonely or bored
It's so cool to be able to naturally talk to the NPCs! Really enjoyed how my suggestions were weaved into the gameplay, and that the characters remembered what I had to say. Highly recommend!
Philosophizing about the cosmos with James is my new favorite past time activity
You get to make the game what you’d like it to be! Duel generative AI!

I loved this game but i did take 2 hours to beat not speedruning it and the npcs are fun how they  can talk and ask you to do diffent stuf like name a brand or help them for a test there is nothing like it also make sure to have your mic on your cant talk to npcs

I love this game just at the start talking to them like they are real people is amazing but I wish there was more maps,characters and more missions because you can only talk to eleven characters in the game so devs if you are reading this can you add more maps and more characters that have a problem of something.
I would give this game a 5 star but it lax replayability, but other then that this is one of a kind of a game, I love talking to the npcs and ordering the most delicious drinks. I recommend this game

An interesting experience where conversations can go just about anywhere. The game took me around 3.5 hours to reach end credits. I had thought going back after end credits would offer more, but it just took me back to the party at the end, but I can't say I was disappointed. Occasionally, the robots gave incorrect information and needed to be corrected, but the seemingly misinformed statements made them seem more human than I expected and them acknowledging their mistakes was fun rather than buggy.  They all had different personalities and I especially enjoyed The Captain. Sadly, no voice credits, but he sounded exactly like 15econdReviews and just kept me in stitches through our conversation. The voice and hand functions work great.
